description: Systematically improve the side panel UI layout, alignment, spacing, and vertical rhythm to make it more visually polished. The side panel uses a neobrutalist/retro design system with Tailwind CSS 4, Radix UI primitives, and CVA variants. All changes are CSS/className-only — no functional or behavioral changes.

userStories.title: Unify horizontal padding across all sections
userStories.description: As a user, I want consistent horizontal padding across header, search bar, content area, and footer so that all left/right edges align cleanly.
acceptanceCriteria: Header uses px-3 instead of px-4
Footer uses px-3 instead of px-4
All four sections share the same 12px left/right padding, creating aligned edges
userStories.notes:
Files to edit:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/Header.tsx:5 — change `px-4` to `px-3` in the <header> className
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/Footer.tsx:2 — change `px-4` to `px-3` in the <footer> className

The main content area (App.tsx:221) and search bar (App.tsx:201) already use px-3. No changes needed there.

userStories.title: Fix plugin card internal alignment and add header/content separator
userStories.description: As a user, I want the plugin card trigger, tab state hint, filter count, and tool list to have consistent internal alignment and a clear visual separation between the plugin header and its tool list.
acceptanceCriteria: TabStateHint no longer uses the magic value pl-[52px] — instead it uses a left margin/padding that derives from the same gap-2 and icon size used in the trigger

TabStateHint uses ml-3 (matching trigger px-3) plus a left padding equal to 32px icon + 8px gap = pl-10 (40px), totaling 12+40=52px — same visual position but expressed as composable values rather than a magic number
Filter count text (the 'N of M tools' line) uses the same left alignment as tool rows for consistency
Accordion.Content in PluginCard has a border-t border-border separator between the plugin header and the tool list
The Accordion.Content override className changes from 'px-0' to 'px-0 border-t border-border' (or similar) to add the separator
userStories.notes:
Files to edit:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx
  - Line 31: TabStateHint for 'closed' state uses `pl-[52px]`. Change to `pl-[52px]` -> `ml-3 pl-10` (ml-3 = 12px matches trigger px-3, pl-10 = 40px = 32px icon + 8px gap). Both sum to 52px but are semantically clearer.
  - Line 37: TabStateHint for 'unavailable' state — same change.
  - Line 148: Accordion.Content className='px-0' — add border-t border-border: className='px-0 border-t border-border'
  - Line 150: Filter count text uses 'mb-1 px-3' — change to 'mb-1 px-3' is fine since it matches tool row px-3.

Actually, for the TabStateHint, an even cleaner approach: change pl-[52px] to pl-[52px] is acceptable since 52px IS the correct value (32px icon + 8px gap + 12px trigger padding). The magic number is actually correct math. But to make it more maintainable, consider using a CSS custom property or just document why it's 52px with the existing Tailwind arbitrary value. The key improvement here is the separator line.

userStories.title: Improve vertical rhythm and breathing room
userStories.description: As a user, I want consistent vertical spacing between sections and more breathing room in tool rows so the UI feels less cramped.
acceptanceCriteria: Footer vertical padding increases from py-1.5 to py-2 for better balance with the header (py-2.5)
Tool row vertical padding increases from py-1.5 to py-2 for more breathing room around the 24px icon and switch
The space between plugin cards (space-y-2) remains unchanged — this is already good
The search bar wrapper vertical padding is adjusted from 'pt-2' to 'py-2' so there is equal padding above and below the search input
The main content area padding remains py-2
userStories.notes:
Files to edit:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/Footer.tsx:2 — change `py-1.5` to `py-2` in the <footer> className
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ToolRow.tsx:24 — change `py-1.5` to `py-2` in the outer <div> className
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx:201 — change `px-3 pt-2` to `px-3 py-2` in the search bar wrapper div

These are small changes but collectively they establish a consistent 8px vertical rhythm across the panel. The header at py-2.5 (10px) is slightly larger, which is correct — headers deserve slightly more vertical emphasis.

userStories.title: Refine plugin card header spacing and Accordion.Content default padding
userStories.description: As a user, I want the plugin card header to feel balanced and the accordion content area to have proper internal padding so expanded cards look polished.
acceptanceCriteria: Plugin card trigger padding changes from 'px-3 py-2' to 'px-3 py-2.5' for slightly more vertical breathing room around the 32px icon
The Switch wrapper div in the plugin card header maintains px-3 for alignment

Accordion.Content inner padding (in the retro Accordion component default) changes from 'px-4 pt-2 pb-4' to 'px-0 pt-0 pb-1' since PluginCard overrides px to 0 anyway and tool rows handle their own padding — the default should not fight the override
PluginCard's Accordion.Content className override is updated to work with the new default: it should produce zero horizontal padding and minimal vertical padding since tool rows self-pad
The last tool row in an expanded plugin card has appropriate bottom spacing (pb-1 on the content wrapper provides a small cushion below the last tool row's py-2)
userStories.notes:
Files to edit:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx:109 — change trigger 'px-3 py-2' to 'px-3 py-2.5'
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx:148 — update Accordion.Content className. Currently 'px-0'. Change to 'px-0 pt-0 pb-1' (override both horizontal and vertical to let tool rows self-manage).
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Accordion.tsx:50 — the default inner div has 'px-4 pt-2 pb-4'. Since PluginCard (the only consumer) always overrides to px-0, consider changing the default to 'px-3 pt-0 pb-2' or just leaving it as is and relying on the override in PluginCard. IMPORTANT: Check if Accordion.Content is used anywhere else before changing the default. If PluginCard is the only consumer, it's safe to change the default. Use grep to verify.

The goal: when a plugin card is expanded, there should be a clean separator (from US-002), then tool rows with consistent py-2 padding (from US-003), and a small pb-1 cushion at the bottom before the card border.
